name: Roadzen Inc.
business: Roadzen is an insurtech company that provides an AI-powered platform for auto insurance, including underwriting, distribution, claims processing, and road safety. It generates revenue through an Insurance as a Service (IaaS) platform (fee-based) and brokerage commissions from embedded and B2B2C insurance distribution. The company sells to insurers, automotive OEMs, fleets, and other distribution channels globally.
sellsTo: Automotive OEMs, dealerships, online car sales platforms
Other distribution channels (financial services, telematics companies)

exposures.sentence: Roadzen is a leading Insurtech company on a mission to transform global auto insurance powered by advanced artificial intelligence (AI).
Roadzen acts as an insurance broker utilizing its technology to sell insurance through our embedded and B2B2C distribution model.
Via : enables fleets, carmakers and insurers to inspect a vehicle using computer vision.
DrivebuddyAI: enables any vehicle to get advanced driver-assistance capabilities utilizing cameras and neural networks to deliver better safety on the road.

Roadzen has four major client types: Insurance including insurance companies, reinsurers, agents, brokers; Automotive including carmakers, dealerships, online-to-offline car sales platforms; Fleets including small and medium fleets, taxi fleets, ridesharing platforms, commercial and corporate fleets; and Other distribution channels such as financial services companies providing auto loans, and telematics companies.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of majority stake in California-based insurance broker and managing general underwriter
catalysts.sentence: We also operate a California-based insurance broker and managing general underwriter, after acquiring a majority stake in that company in the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Global automotive insurance market was $923 billion in 2023, projected to reach $2.3 trillion by 2032 (10.8% CAGR).
tamClaims.sentence: The global automotive insurance market was approximately $923 billion in 2023 and is projected to reach approximately $2.3 trillion by 2032, growing at a compounded annual rate of 10.8%, according to Allied Market Research.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1.55 billion vehicles on the road worldwide (excluding motorcycles).
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 1.55 billion vehicles on the road worldwide, including cars, vans and trucks (excluding motorcycles), according to Organisation Internationale des Constructeurs d Automobiles (OICA).
